stories.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
stories.simple_summary: A successful investor thinks the huge amounts of money being made from AI will eventually need to be shared more widely among people, either by choice or by force.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and wealthy people — nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company wants to sell an expensive AI assistant (a computer helper) built into a fancy foldable phone for nearly $7,000.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you're interested in expensive gadgets, this doesn't affect you — it's a product for people with very high budgets.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: A company called Databricks is now worth $188 billion because it helps businesses use AI, especially for writing computer code.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: What to know about the AI chip stock selloff - ABC News
stories.source: Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.simple_summary: The stock prices of companies that make AI computer chips have dropped recently.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Dell Technologies vs. NVIDIA: Which Artificial Intelligence Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
stories.simple_summary: Financial advisors are comparing two big tech companies to decide which is a better investment for buying their stock.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: An AI feature on Zoom can automatically record, transcribe (convert speech to text), and summarize meetings, but some people worry about privacy and whether anyone will actually read all this information.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom for business meetings, this matters — you may want to check your settings to control whether your meetings are being recorded and summarized.

stories.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
stories.simple_summary: A robotics company is opening a training center in California for its robot named Digit, in the same area where Tesla operates.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to engineers and robotics companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
stories.simple_summary: Companies making smartphones in India are struggling because AI features are making phones more expensive and people aren't buying them as much.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're shopping for a new phone, prices may stay higher because companies are adding AI features — you might want to wait or look for simpler models.

stories.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
stories.simple_summary: TikTok is testing a tool that can spot fake videos made by AI that pretend to be real people, and lets creators report them.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use TikTok or create videos, this could help you know if someone is using AI to impersonate you or spread fake videos of you.

stories.title: China’s Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi Model, Threatening America’s Lead
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI company has released a new AI model, which some people see as competition to American AI companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech companies and investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: MLB restricts using dugout iPads for AI-assisted in-game strategy
stories.simple_summary: Major League Baseball is preventing baseball teams from using AI-powered suggestions on their iPads to make playing decisions during games.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're a baseball fan, this means games will be decided by human strategy rather than AI advice — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
stories.simple_summary: Apple sued OpenAI claiming the company stole secret information and hired over 400 of Apple's former employees, which could cause problems if OpenAI tries to sell shares to the public soon.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters to investors — nothing you need to act on, though it shows that big tech companies are fighting over AI talent and ideas.
